title: One p-first symplectic Euler step preserves a modified quadratic invariant

source.citation: Ernst Hairer, Christian Lubich, and Gerhard Wanner, Geometric Numerical Integration, 2nd ed., Springer, 2006, Ch. VI, pp. 179-236.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For real omega, dt, q, and p, define pNext = p - dt omega^2 q and qNext = q + dt pNext. Then the quantity p^2 + omega^2 q^2 - dt omega^2 q p has the same value at (qNext, pNext) as at (q, p).
informal.latex: Let \(\omega,\Delta t,q,p\in\mathbb{R}\). Define \(p_{+}=p-\Delta t\,\omega^2q\) and \(q_{+}=q+\Delta t\,p_{+}\). Then \(p_{+}^2+\omega^2q_{+}^2-\Delta t\,\omega^2q_{+}p_{+}=p^2+\omega^2q^2-\Delta t\,\omega^2qp\).
assumptions: omega, dt, q, and p are real numbers
pNext is defined by the p-first symplectic Euler momentum update
qNext is defined by the p-first symplectic Euler position update
informal.expected_conclusion: The modified quadratic invariant is preserved by one p-first symplectic Euler step.

declarations.name: symplecticEulerPNext
declarations.statement: def symplecticEulerPNext (omega dt q p : ℝ) : ℝ := p - dt * omega ^ 2 * q

declarations.name: symplecticEulerQNext
declarations.statement: def symplecticEulerQNext (omega dt q p : ℝ) : ℝ := q + dt * symplecticEulerPNext omega dt q p

declarations.name: symplecticEulerInvariant
declarations.statement: def symplecticEulerInvariant (omega dt q p : ℝ) : ℝ := p ^ 2 + omega ^ 2 * q ^ 2 - dt * omega ^ 2 * q * p

declarations.name: symplecticEulerInvariant_preserved
declarations.statement: theorem symplecticEulerInvariant_preserved (omega dt q p : ℝ) : symplecticEulerInvariant omega dt (symplecticEulerQNext omega dt q p) (symplecticEulerPNext omega dt q p) = symplecticEulerInvariant omega dt q p

tasks.input: Translate the one-step p-first symplectic Euler invariant statement into a Lean 4 theorem using the provided definitions.
tasks.target: theorem symplecticEulerInvariant_preserved (omega dt q p : ℝ) : symplecticEulerInvariant omega dt (symplecticEulerQNext omega dt q p) (symplecticEulerPNext omega dt q p) = symplecticEulerInvariant omega dt q p

tasks.input: Given the theorem statement and definitions, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using unfold and ring.
